meta.title: Wat Phnom Daun Penh - Phnom Penh Historic Temple Guide
meta.description: Discover Wat Phnom Daun Penh, one of Phnom Penh's most famous Buddhist landmarks. Learn its history, Khmer architecture, visiting tips and how to get there.

hero.localName: វត្តភ្នំដូនពេញ
hero.title: Wat Phnom Daun Penh
hero.subtitle: One of Phnom Penh's most representative historic Buddhist landmarks, perched on the city's central hill.
hero.legend: According to local legend, the temple was founded after Lady Penh discovered four Buddha statues inside a floating koki tree washed up on the riverbank.
hero.heroAlt: Wat Phnom Daun Penh temple standing on its hill in central Phnom Penh, Cambodia

intro.title: About Wat Phnom Daun Penh
intro.description: Wat Phnom Daun Penh is among the most famous historic temples in Phnom Penh, sitting atop a small hill in the heart of the city. It is both an important Buddhist site and a cultural landmark tied to the origin of the city's name.

highlights.title: Why it matters
items: The legendary origin of Phnom Penh's name
A living Buddhist temple still active today
Panoramic views over the city centre
Khmer art, murals and sacred relics

guide.title: Plan your visit
guide.description: A compact hilltop site — easy to combine with other central Phnom Penh attractions.
items: Allow 30–60 minutes for a relaxed visit
Dress respectfully when entering the temple
Combine with the Riverside and Royal Palace area
Visit early morning for cooler weather and fewer crowds

history.title: History of Wat Phnom
history.subtitle: From a 14th-century legend to the symbolic heart of Phnom Penh.

sections.heading: The legend of Lady Penh
sections.body: Local tradition tells of a wealthy widow, Daun Penh (Grandmother Penh), who found four Buddha statues in a koki tree carried by the Mekong floodwaters. She raised a small hill ('phnom') and built a shrine — giving the city its name, Phnom Penh ('Hill of Penh').

sections.heading: A growing sacred hill
sections.body: The shrine was rebuilt and expanded over the centuries. The artificial hill became a focal point of local devotion, and the surrounding settlement grew into the capital we know today.

sections.heading: Renovation under the French Protectorate
sections.body: Successive kings, especially during the 19th century, restored and enlarged the temple. The current stupa and vihara reflect later Khmer restoration styles.

sections.heading: Symbol of the capital
sections.body: Today Wat Phnom stands as a national cultural symbol and the namesake hill of Phnom Penh, welcoming both worshippers and visitors from around the world.

history.legendTitle: The founding legend of Daun Penh
history.legendText: The story of Lady Penh and the floating koki tree is the most beloved origin tale of Phnom Penh. It explains not only the temple's founding but also how the capital came to be called 'Phnom Penh' — the Hill of Penh.

architecture.title: Khmer Buddhist Architecture
architecture.subtitle: A layered hilltop sanctuary of stupas, shrines, murals and sacred trees.

features.title: The Central Stupa
features.text: A tall whitewashed stupa houses sacred relics and marks the spiritual centre of the hill.

features.title: Buddha Statues
features.text: Revered Buddha images, including statues linked to the founding legend, are enshrined in the vihara.

features.title: Wall Murals
features.text: Vibrant murals depict the Jataka tales and scenes from Buddhist cosmology around the shrine.

features.title: Khmer Ornament
features.text: Naga balustrades, lotus motifs and carved lintels show classic Khmer decorative craft.

features.title: Sacred Trees & Shrine
features.text: Shady trees and a small shrine to Lady Penh frame the peaceful summit.

features.title: Hilltop Setting
features.text: The artificial hill lifts the temple above the street, offering calm city views.

gallery.title: Moments at Wat Phnom
gallery.subtitle: A visual walk through the temple, its art and the surrounding city.
gallery.viewMaps: View Wat Phnom on Google Maps

experience.title: What Visitors Can Experience
experience.subtitle: A short but rich stop for culture, calm and city views.

items.title: Visit the Buddhist shrine
items.text: Observe local devotion and learn about everyday Buddhist practice at the vihara.

items.title: Enjoy the city view
items.text: Look out over central Phnom Penh from the top of the hill.

items.title: Photograph the details
items.text: Ideal for architecture and cultural photography — stupa, murals and ornament.

items.title: Feel local life
items.text: Watch residents come to pray, make offerings and seek blessings.

bestTime.title: When to Visit Wat Phnom
bestTime.subtitle: The hilltop is open daily; each part of the day offers a different mood.

day.label: Morning
day.text: Cooler weather and fewer visitors — a peaceful time for photography and prayer.

day.label: Afternoon
day.text: Warm, directional light is great for capturing the architecture and murals.

day.label: Evening
day.text: The city lights up and the hilltop feels atmospheric as the day winds down.

bestTime.note: The temple is busiest mid-morning and on weekends; arrive early for a quieter experience.

transport.title: How to Get to Wat Phnom
transport.subtitle: Wat Phnom is in central Phnom Penh, easy to reach from most areas of the city.
transport.overview: The hill sits near the intersection of Norodom Boulevard and Street 96, a short ride from the Riverside and Royal Palace.

modes.title: Tuk Tuk
modes.description: The most common way to get around Phnom Penh.
details: Flexible and convenient for short trips
Fares vary by distance and time of day
Agree on a price before you ride

modes.title: Taxi / App
modes.description: Comfortable for families and hotter days.
details: Metered taxis and ride-hailing apps available
Direct drop-off near the hill entrance

modes.title: On Foot
modes.description: Walkable from the central Riverside area.
details: Combine with a stroll along the river

map.title: Find Wat Phnom on the Map
map.subtitle: Central Phnom Penh, on the hill at the end of Wat Phnom Street.
map.addressValue: Wat Phnom Road, Phnom Penh, Cambodia

nearby.title: Explore Around Wat Phnom
nearby.subtitle: Region-level highlights within easy reach of the hill — no specific hotels or businesses recommended.

places.name: Royal Palace Area
places.text: The royal compound and Silver Pagoda — Phnom Penh's most important royal and cultural district.

places.name: Riverside (Tonlé Sap & Mekong)
places.text: A breezy promenade along the confluence of the rivers, perfect for walking and city views.

places.name: Central Market (Phsar Thmei)
places.text: A striking art-deco market showcasing local commerce and daily Phnom Penh life.

food.title: Food Culture Near Wat Phnom
food.subtitle: A taste of Cambodian cuisine — cultural context, not restaurant recommendations.
food.intro: Around the hill you'll find classic Cambodian flavours. This is a cultural introduction to what locals eat, not an endorsement of specific venues.

items.name: Khmer Breakfast
items.text: Rice porridge (babor) and fresh baguettes reflect Cambodia's blend of local and French-influenced mornings.

items.name: Cambodian Curry
items.text: Kari sach moan — a gentle coconut curry with chicken, potato and lemongrass.

items.name: Grilled Meats
items.text: Skewered and grilled meats sold at street stalls and local eateries.

items.name: Street Snacks
items.text: Nom kong (palm-sugar cookies) and fried noodles are everyday favourites.

items.name: Iced Khmer Coffee
items.text: Strong coffee with condensed milk — a staple of Cambodian café culture.

practical.title: Travel Essentials
practical.subtitle: Everything you need for a smooth, respectful visit.

cards.title: Opening Hours
cards.text: Open daily from 07:00 to 19:00. Mornings are coolest and quietest.

cards.title: Admission
cards.text: Check on-site for the latest entry information; local customs apply.

cards.title: Suggested Stay
cards.text: Plan 30–60 minutes for the hilltop, shrine and views.

cards.title: Who It's For
cards.text: Culture lovers, photographers and families exploring central Phnom Penh.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for planning your visit to Wat Phnom.

items.a: Yes — it is one of Phnom Penh's most important cultural landmarks and the namesake hill of the city.

items.q: How long should I spend at Wat Phnom?
items.a: Most visitors spend 30 minutes to 1 hour exploring the hilltop, shrine and views.
Respectful clothing that covers shoulders and knees is recommended when visiting the temple.
In central Phnom Penh, Cambodia, on the hill at the end of Wat Phnom Street.

items.q: When is the best time to visit?
items.a: Early morning for cooler weather and fewer crowds; late afternoon for warm light and city atmosphere.

footer.tagline: A free, multilingual cultural guide to Phnom Penh's historic hilltop temple.
footer.rights: This is an independent information guide, not affiliated with any temple, business or government body.
footer.missionText: We organize verified facts, history and practical visitor information into a free, language-friendly guide — without commercial bookings or recommendations.
footer.addressValue: Wat Phnom Road, Phnom Penh, Cambodia
footer.disclaimer: Photos are used for illustrative purposes. Verify opening hours and admission on-site before your visit.
